messages.content:
{
  "goal": "Resolve the customer ticket end-to-end using only the approved tools.",
  "ticket": {
    "ticket_id": "TICK-2002",
    "customer_id": "CUST-SYN-2002",
    "subject": "I was charged twice for the same invoice",
    "body": "I was billed twice for invoice INV-9979 yesterday. Please explain the duplicate charge and what refund steps apply.",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "escalated",
    "issue_type": "billing",
    "resolution_summary": "Escalation required. Classification: billing / resolve_duplicate_charge (0.90 confidence). Latest customer message: \"I checked again and both charges settled on the card statement.\". Evidence reviewed: KB-110 matched both, charged, charges, invoice; KB-111 matched card, invoice; KB-114 matched invoice. KB attempts: \"I was charged twice for the same invoice I checked again and both charges settled on the card statement.\" -> Retrieved 3 KB article(s).. Draft assessment: \"Thanks for confirming both charges settled. This needs a billing correction and refund review, so I'm escalating this to the billing team for manual handling.\". Escalation trigger: draft_response failed with 'Draft indicates escalation is required.'.",
    "customer_message_history": [
      "I checked again and both charges settled on the card statement."
    ],
    "pending_customer_replies": [],
    "response_history": [
      "Priority noted. Please share the invoice IDs and payment timestamps so we can confirm whether the second charge is only a temporary authorization or a true duplicate. If both charges have settled, we will correct the billing and confirm the refund timeline.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here.",
      "Thanks for the additional details. I'm escalating this to a billing specialist now and including the conversation history and the steps already attempted so they can continue from here."
    ],
    "tool_failures": {
      "search_kb": 0,
      "post_response": 0
    }
  }
}

messages.name: classify_ticket
messages.content: {"message": "Ticket classified as billing with high priority.", "outcome": "success", "data": {"issue_type": "billing", "intent": "resolve_duplicate_charge", "confidence": "high", "confidence_score": 0.9, "priority": "high", "in_scope": true}}

arguments.query: I was charged twice for the same invoice I checked again and both charges settled on the card statement.

messages.name: search_kb
messages.content: {"message": "Retrieved 3 KB article(s).", "outcome": "success", "data": {"query": "I was charged twice for the same invoice I checked again and both charges settled on the card statement.", "issue_type": "billing", "intent": "resolve_duplicate_charge", "classification_confidence": 0.9, "grounding_confidence": 1.0, "information_types": ["policy", "policy", "policy"], "hits": [{"article_id": "KB-110", "title": "Resolve duplicate invoice charges", "issue_type": "billing", "information_type": "policy", "summary": "Verify the invoice IDs, confirm whether the second charge is a pending authorization, and issue a billing correction if both settled.", "matched_terms": ["both", "charged", "charges", "invoice", "settled"], "score": 25.0, "confidence_score": 1.0}, {"article_id": "KB-111", "title": "Resolve failed renewal or card decline", "issue_type": "billing", "information_type": "policy", "summary": "Renewal failures usually come from card declines, expired payment methods, or bank authorization blocks.", "matched_terms": ["card", "invoice"], "score": 11.5, "confidence_score": 1.0}, {"article_id": "KB-114", "title": "Send invoice or receipt copies", "issue_type": "billing", "information_type": "policy", "summary": "Invoice and receipt requests require the billing email and the invoice period or transaction date before the document can be located.", "matched_terms": ["invoice"], "score": 10.5, "confidence_score": 1.0}]}}

function.name: read_ticket
function.description: Load the current ticket details and customer context.

function.name: classify_ticket
function.description: Classify the ticket into a supported issue type, infer user intent, and estimate confidence.

function.name: search_kb
function.description: Search the approved knowledge base for a grounded resolution path.

issue_type.description: The classified issue type when available.

intent.description: The inferred user intent for the ticket.

classification_confidence.description: Confidence score from the intent classifier.

limit.description: Maximum number of articles to return.

function.name: draft_response
function.description: Draft a customer-facing reply using the ticket and retrieved KB evidence.

article_ids.description: The KB article identifiers used as evidence.

function.name: post_response
function.description: Post the drafted response back to the ticket.

citations.description: KB article identifiers cited in the reply.

function.name: update_ticket_status
function.description: Update the ticket status after resolution or handoff.

resolution_summary.description: A short summary of the resolution or handoff.

function.name: escalate_ticket
function.description: Escalate the ticket with a clear explanation if grounded resolution fails.

reason.description: Why the ticket must be escalated.
